verdict_reasoning: HMGB1 is canonically a nuclear DNA-binding protein, but has a well-documented non-baseline surface pool. Under cell-stress conditions (inflammation, infection, hypoxia, oxidative stress) or during cell death (necrosis, necroptosis, pyroptosis), HMGB1 translocates to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane and is released extracellularly as a damage-associated molecular pattern (DAMP). This cell-state-induced ecto-display is a major biological axis — HMGB1 acts as a TLR4 / TLR2 / RAGE ligand from the extracellular space and is central to sterile inflammation. Surface-biotinylation and cell-surface immunofluorescence studies document outer-leaflet presence during stress and cell death. At baseline in healthy cells, HMGB1 remains nuclear and cytoplasmic; the surface pool is non-baseline.
